Center: US National Nuclear Data Center, Brookhaven, USA
ref: Jour: Physical Review, Part C, Nuclear Physics, Vol.109, p.064611 (2024)
title: Measurement of the prompt fission neutron spectrum from 800 keV to 10 MeV for 240Pu(sf) and for the 240Pu(n,F) reaction induced by neutrons of energy from 1-20 MeV

x4freetext: Measurement of the prompt fission neutron spectrum
from 800 keV to 10 MeV for 240Pu(sf) and for the
240Pu(n,F) reaction induced by neutrons of energy
from 1-20 MeV

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Physical Review, Part C, Nuclear Physics, Vol.109, p.064611 (2024)

flag.hlp: Reference to related data compiled in another entry

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Physical Review, Part C, Nuclear Physics, Vol.102, p.034615 (2020)

x4codes.hlp: Los Alamos National Laboratory, NM, United States of America
Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, Livermore, CA, United States of America
Los Alamos National Laboratory, NM, United States of America

x4freetext: The high-energy Los Alamos Neutron
Science Center white neutron source at the Weapons
Neutron Research facility.
Neutrons were detected with a 54-element
EJ-309 liquid scintillator detector array Chi-Nu.
A parallel plate avalanche
counter (PPAC) target, fabricated at Lawrence
Livermore National Laboratory, containing
approximately 20 mg total of 99.875% pure 240Pu
located within the experimental flight path (WNR
FP15L). The remaining contributions to the total
target mass are 0.114% 242Pu, with all other isotopes
below 0.01%.
Incident neutrons from 1.0-20.0 MeV were
measured via time of flight between a signal
corresponding to the spallation reaction time,
denoted t0, and a fission detection in the PPAC at a
time tf. The incident neutron flight path was
21.5(1) m, yielding flight times from 1483.94-281.39
ns for Einc n = 1.0-20.0 MeV.
Error propagation was used to calculate total
uncertainties from statistical and systematic.
Corrections were applied to both the (n,f) and (sf)
liquid scintillator and PPAC data to eliminate signal
reflections within the cables, signal time walk, timing
alignments of all detectors together, and to isolate
neutron signals from gamma rays using pulse-shape
discrimination (PSD), and further purify the neutron
signals using neutron kinematics relative to the
fission detection times.
BP
BP: Updated data per K.J.Kelley request
in subentries 2-21.
